newsSinceJun6: No material news since June 6. Only fresh item is a soft PR (named to Inc.'s 2026 Best Workplaces, Logistics & Transportation) - immaterial. The known negatives (May 7 dilutive $100M raise at $6.50/share that sent the stock down 37%, and the Atlas 100-truck order ramp pushed to 2027) predate June 6 and were reflected in yesterday's down verdict. Long-haul driverless launch milestone still targeted for Sept 1, 2026. Today's -4.2% is drift/flow, below the 5% threshold, with no identifiable catalyst.

sources.label: StockTitan - Kodiak AI named to Inc. 2026 Best Workplaces
TechCrunch - Kodiak AI raises $100M at steep discount, stock tumbles 37% (May 7, 2026)
Seeking Alpha - Kodiak AI: Major Delay And Dilution (Q1)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's down move already priced the dilution and Atlas ramp delay; nothing genuinely new since June 6, and today's -4.2% is drift, not a thesis event. Continued de-crowding at $1.33B cap with the Sept 2026 driverless launch catalyst intact marginally improves asymmetry, so hold with slight positive drift rather than another cut.
verdicts.bull: A still-funded pure-play on real-world autonomy at -43% YTD with a dated, binary catalyst (long-haul driverless launch targeted Sept 1, 2026) plus Bosch hardware and General Dynamics defense optionality - asymmetry improves as the tape washes out holders.
verdicts.neutral: TTM revenue of ~$3.8M against a ~$585M net loss means the stock trades entirely on milestone credibility; until the Atlas ramp or ARM completion shows progress, it is a proxy position, and the robotics-page thesis (autonomy under industrial constraints) neither strengthens nor weakens this week.
verdicts.bear: The May raise at $6.50 reset warrant/convertible strikes and expanded the future share count; with the 100-truck revenue ramp pushed to 2027, further dilution before commercialization is the base case, and short interest is rising sharply.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Long-haul driverless launch / ARM completion targeted around September 1, 2026, plus visible Atlas truck deliveries scaling via Roush.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Another dilutive capital raise or a slip in the H2 2026 driverless launch, which would break the milestone-credibility thesis at a near-zero-revenue valuation.
